mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS below the prior 3-month average reduces discount rates and raises equity present values, so S&P 500 20-day forward returns turn positive after easing begins. If rates are flat or rising, the effect is absent.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing episodes do not deliver higher 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the sign of the effect is negative in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL acceleration above the recent trend forces tighter policy expectations and raises real discount rates, which compresses equity multiples and lowers S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when inflation prints are stable or falling.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above its 12-month moving average by 0.5pp
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-acceleration triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if conditional Rank IC is not negative over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises while PAYEMS growth slows, investors infer a weakening labor market and higher recession risk, causing S&P 500 forward returns to be negative over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if unemployment rises without payroll deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration signal does not outperform either proxy alone and 60-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal consumer demand and better near-term revenue expectations, supporting S&P 500 gains over the next 20 trading days. The effect weakens when sales growth is being offset by higher inflation or tightening policy.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum does not coincide with above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO with stable labor conditions signals broad output expansion without immediate macro stress, which supports cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The signal fails when production gains come from inflationary overheating or labor-market weakening.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and UNRATE does not rise more than 0.2pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint production-strength condition is not associated with higher 60-day SPX returns than the unconditional mean, or if the conditional IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent range signals housing contraction and weaker downstream demand, which pulls S&P 500 returns lower over the next 5 trading days. The effect disappears when housing weakness is offset by easing financial conditions.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 8% below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-drawdown triggers are followed by nonnegative 5-day SPX returns in most cases or if the average triggered return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A restrictive combination of FEDFUNDS above neutral and CPIAUCSL still elevated keeps real policy tight, compressing equity valuations and lowering S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is not present when inflation has already fallen back toward target.
failure_condition: Reject if the restrictive policy-and-inflation regime does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than other regimes, or if the interaction term is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE signals resilient income and spending, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment is stable but payrolls contract materially.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of stable low unemployment do not deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect becomes negative in any 3-year rolling window.
